steps.title: Track the text's purpose
steps.content: The passage begins with the problem of getting rid of weeds, then proposes a surprising solution: eating edible plants that are usually considered weeds.

steps.title: Reject narrower readings
steps.content: Choice B is too broad because the text does not explain gardening methods. Choice C treats flavor as the main point even though it is only supporting detail. Choice D invents a cooking trend.

steps.title: Name the solution structure
steps.content: Choice A captures the passage's actual purpose: it suggests an unexpected solution to the weed problem by pointing out that some weeds are edible and flavorful. The answer is A.
